topic: RN chances in Marseille, Lyon, Toulouse 2026 municipal elections
summary: Comprehensive polling and market data for RN's chances in the three target cities
content:
## Elections: March 15 (round 1) and March 22 (round 2), 2026

### MARSEILLE (Best RN chance)
- First round polls: Payan (DVG) 30-31%, Allisio (RN) 27-31%, Vassal (LR-RE) 20-23%, Delogu (LFI) 13-15%
- Four candidates could qualify for second round (quadrangulaire)
- Second round polls (OpinionWay Jan 2026):
  - Triangulaire (Delogu-Payan-Allisio): Payan 43%, Allisio 42% (very tight!)
  - Triangulaire with Vassal: Vassal 55%, Allisio 45%
  - Cluster17 Nov 2025: Payan 45%, Vassal 25%, Allisio 30%
- Kalshi market: Payan 85%, Allisio 11%, Vassal 1%
- Key: Vassal has said she WILL maintain in second round (quadrangulaire)
- Vassal refuses formal alliance with RN but has been ambiguous
- RN VP Sébastien Chenu: "Marseille is within our reach"
- Politico: "A National Rally win here would rank among the biggest victories in the history of the French far right"

### LYON (Very low RN chance)
- Jean-Michel Aulas (LR-RE-HOR) dominates: 47% first round
- RN candidate Alexandre Dupalais: only 6-8%
- Polymarket: Aulas 94% to win
- RN is a non-factor in Lyon

### TOULOUSE (Very low RN chance)  
- Moudenc (incumbent, formerly LR) leads: 33-34%
- Briançon (PS-PCF-LE): 30-32%
- Piquemal (LFI): 19%
- RN candidate Leonardelli: only 8%
- RN is a non-factor in Toulouse

### KEY STRUCTURAL FACTOR
French two-round municipal elections historically feature "front républicain" - other parties unite against FN/RN in the second round. However, Marseille is showing unusual dynamics with a possible 4-way second round where the anti-RN vote is split.

### MARKET ASSESSMENT
Kalshi gives Allisio only 11% chance in Marseille. This is the ONLY city where RN has any realistic chance.
